texto: He dropped the tavern apron in a heap on the floor and pulled the freshly laundered one up and over his head, tied it with slightly tremoring fingers. The vast whiteness felt like absolution.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Devon Trevarrow Flaherty: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “He dropped the tavern apron in a heap on the floor and pulled the freshly laundered one up and ov…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, guilt, dropped pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: He dropped the tavern apron in a heap on the floor and pulled the freshly laundered one up and over his head, tied it with slightly tremoring fingers. The vast whiteness felt like absolution.

texto: You stupid cow!” He laughed at her behind those icicle eyes. “You really think anyone is better than you? Everyone’s a thief, woman! Waiting for the next terrible thing to do to get ahead of their neighbor. Forty of us around a table weren’t enough to convince you of that?
